insight: Diverging in-state and out-of-state acceptance rates, alongside an increasingly active waitlist (admitting 500 students for the Class of 2029 compared to just 15 the year prior).
implication: Out-of-state applicants face a significantly stiffer statistical disadvantage (~54% acceptance) compared to in-state residents (~76%), and the university is actively utilizing its waitlist to manage yield amidst record application numbers (>52,000).
